title: Brunette
content:
When trees in Spring
    Are blossoming
    My lady wakes
    From dreams whose light
    Made dark days bright,
    For their sweet sakes.

    Yet in her eyes
    A shadow lies
    Of bygone mirth;
    And still she seems
    To walk in dreams,
    And not on earth.

    Some men may hold
    That hair of gold
    Is lovelier
    Than darker sheen:
    They have not seen
    My lady�s hair.

    Her eyes are bright,
    Her bosom white
    As the sea foam
    On sharp rocks sprayed;
    Her mouth is made
    Of honeycomb.

    And whoso seeks
    In her dusk cheeks
    May see Love�s sign,
    A blush that glows
    Like a red rose
    Beneath brown wine.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Victor James Daley delivers a powerful performance in "Brunette"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
